texto: God the Father wants us to know Him. We can only know Him through Christ, and our relationship with Christ brings forth eternal life! You see Christ is our salvation. Salvation is notsomething outside of Him, He is our salvation Himself.

texto: The reality is that we are in the hands of an angry God, who in His abounding mercy and love has given us a way out of what we so rightly deserve; Hell, the Lake of Fire and endless eternities without His presence!
explicacao: Citação de Billy Witt sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The reality is that we are in the hands of an angry God, who in His abounding mercy and love has …” articula reflexao, mercy, reality em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

frase_original: The reality is that we are in the hands of an angry God, who in His abounding mercy and love has given us a way out of what we so rightly deserve; Hell, the Lake of Fire and endless eternities without His presence!

texto: And Jesus began to say to them, “See to it that no one misleads you. Many will come in My name, saying, ‘I am He!’ and will mislead many.”Here Christ cautions His present and future disciples from being misled, meaning not to be deceived, caused to err from the truth, or lead to form a wrong judgment. It is important for us to note that we can be deceived if we heed deceivers, instead of Christ Jesus. Many claim and will claim to know Him, but will point us to themselves, to traditions, to lies, and anything instead of to Christ! We must be careful.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Billy Witt sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “And Jesus began to say to them, “See to it that no one misleads you. Many will come in My name, s…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, deception, heavy-fog, útil em momentos de heavy-fog e lies.

frase_original: And Jesus began to say to them, “See to it that no one misleads you. Many will come in My name, saying, ‘I am He!’ and will mislead many.”Here Christ cautions His present and future disciples from being misled, meaning not to be deceived, caused to err from the truth, or lead to form a wrong judgment. It is important for us to note that we can be deceived if we heed deceivers, instead of Christ Jesus. Many claim and will claim to know Him, but will point us to themselves, to traditions, to lies, and anything instead of to Christ! We must be careful.
